data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Increased pressure from displaced populations across the eastern Mediterranean generated Sea Peoples raiding fleets that systematically attacked Mycenaean, Hittite, Ugaritic and Levantine coastal centers, precipitating their collapse.", "mechanism": "external demographic pressure leading to maritime raider incursions", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city‑states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Archaeological layers at major coastal sites lack evidence of widespread destruction or foreign weaponry dating to 1200–1150 BCE", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "Analytical matrix compares timing, intensity and geographic spread of raids across the four societies."}
